title: A net-new leaf can title two tests with the SAME contract id, silently dropping a declared contract's coverage while the signed --real green still PASSes
description: Building library-dive-body (inc 4 of the library-tech-tree-overlay arc), the frontend leaf authored all 6 declared ldb- behaviours but titled the pure planDive(null) test with an ADJACENT contract's id (ldb-empty-state-no-selection, duplicating the component test) instead of its own (ldb-plandive-empty-on-null). The signed --real green still PASSed (it attests ONE authored test, ADR-0020 3), so the miss only surfaced at the post-PASS coverage check.
statement: When a net-new leaf authors N contracts in one real.testFile, it can title two distinct tests with the SAME contract id (reusing an adjacent id) — the id-name coverage scan then counts the reused id once and reports the shadowed contract UNCOVERED, even though a substantive test for it exists. The signed --real verdict PASSes regardless (it observes one red-green, not the per-contract ledger), so a genuinely-tested-but-mislabeled contract reads as a coverage gap only at the orchestrator's post-PASS `storytree coverage` step.
evidence: Verdict: run real-mrgh3sd4 signed PASS @ 7fc59e4 printed `coverage 5/6 contracts (⚠ uncovered: ldb-plandive-empty-on-null)`. The authored apps/studio/src/components/LibraryDiveBody.test.tsx had `it('ldb-empty-state-no-selection: a null selection plans the empty state', () => expect(planDive(null)).toEqual({kind:'empty'}))` at line 98 AND `it('ldb-empty-state-no-selection: with no selection renders a bare pick-an-artifact prompt …')` at line 127 — the line-98 test is substantively the ldb-plandive-empty-on-null contract, mislabeled with the line-127 id. `storytree coverage library-dive-body` reported 5/6 (exit 1). Fix was a one-word title correction (no assertion/source change) → 6 tests green, coverage 6/6. RECURRENCE (2nd occurrence, inc 5 of the same arc, branch claude/real/library-overview-real-mrgxl3g2, 2026-07-12): run real-mrgxl3g2 signed PASS @ 0730e31 printed `coverage 4/8 contracts` — the leaf RENAMED (not duplicated) 4 of 8 declared lov- ids to richer variants it preferred (e.g. declared `lov-size-tier-buckets-by-importance` → authored `lov-size-tier-monotonic-three-buckets`; `lov-lod-band-by-zoom`→`lov-lod-band-thresholds-monotonic`; `lov-layout-total-and-deterministic`→`lov-layout-totality-and-determinism-cycle-tolerant`; `lov-far-band-one-element-per-node`→`…-shape-by-category`) plus an EXTRA `lov-close-band-arc-plaque-reads-epic`. All 8 behaviours were substantively tested — only the id tokens diverged from the spec's declared contracts. `storytree coverage library-overview` reported 4/8; fix was a title-only alignment of the 4 tokens (impl byte-identical to 0730e31) → 8/8, 15 vitest tests still green. So the class is broader than DUPLICATE ids: a leaf freely RENAMES declared ids, and the signed --real PASS never binds test titles to the spec's contract ledger.
impact: Cost a coverage-debug + a consolidation title-fix commit before the gate could go green; the signed verdict text now permanently records 5/6 (and, on the recurrence, 4/8) while the landed code is N/N (a ledger delta the PR/arc-log had to explain). Hits any orchestrator routing a multi-contract net-new leaf through --real: a mislabeled/duplicated/RENAMED contract id passes the signed green but reds check:coverage, invisible until the post-PASS coverage step. Twice now on the same arc — the signed --real leaf does not bind its authored test titles to the spec's declared contract ids, so post-PASS title reconciliation is a recurring orchestrator tax. Candidate routes: a leaf-facing guardrail/process that pins each authored test title to a declared contract id, or a coverage check folded INTO the --real gate so a mislabel reds the sign rather than riding past it.
